# Bootstrap Requirements Checklist: Bootstrap Argo CD With Terragrunt

**Purpose**: Validate that bootstrap, GitOps handoff, secret/input, and recovery requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and ready for implementation planning
**Created**: 2026-05-24
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the Terragrunt bootstrap ownership requirements complete for install, self-management registration, and post-handoff boundaries? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-003a]
- [x] CHK002 Are all repo-owned desired-state paths identified for the bootstrap stack, catalog modules, and Argo CD steady-state configuration? [Completeness, Spec §IAC-001, Plan §Project Structure]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements defined for both the initial seed path and the later Argo CD-owned steady-state path? [Coverage, Spec §US1, Spec §US2]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements present for pinning catalog module sources and chart versions before rollout? [Gap, Spec §FR-002, Research §Source The Terragrunt Catalog Module Remotely, Data Model §Argo CD Installation]
- [x] CHK005 Are documentation requirements complete for prerequisites, apply path, healthy state, validation, rollback, and partial-bootstrap recovery? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Quickstart §Prerequisites]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "one documented Terragrunt apply path" defined with a specific entry point and scope boundary? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Infrastructure Entry Point]
- [x] CHK007 Is the phrase "after successful handoff" clarified with observable handoff states or criteria? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-003a, Data Model §Self-Management Application]
- [x] CHK008 Is "repo-owned desired-state path" specified enough to prevent confusion between Terragrunt module paths and Argo CD source paths? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Plan §Repository Paths]
- [x] CHK009 Are "external secret material" and "CI/CD-injected credentials" distinguished from committed non-secret inputs? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK010 Is the initial handoff mode clearly described as manual validation before automated prune/self-heal? [Clarity, Research §Validate First Handoff, Contract §Expected Outputs]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Do the spec and plan consistently state that Terragrunt seeds Argo CD while Argo CD owns steady-state configuration after handoff? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK012 Do secret/input requirements align across functional requirements, infrastructure requirements, and the operator contract? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Spec §IAC-006, Contract §Committed Inputs]
- [x] CHK013 Are validation requirements consistent between the spec success criteria, plan testing context, and quickstart validation sequence? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Plan §Testing, Quickstart §Validate Before Apply]
- [x] CHK014 Are ingress assumptions consistent across the spec, research decisions, and planned Helm/service exposure requirements? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Research §No Public Ingress]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Are the success criteria measurable without relying on hidden implementation details? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001 through §SC-006]
- [x] CHK016 Can the 10-minute Argo CD self-management outcome be objectively assessed from documented signals? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK017 Is "one operator workflow" defined well enough to judge whether manual sequencing has leaked into the requirements? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-002, Research §One Composite Terragrunt Entry Point]
- [x] CHK018 Are duplicate-resource prevention requirements measurable for repeated bootstrap attempts? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are primary, alternate, and recovery scenarios all represented for first install, steady-state handoff, and partial failure? [Coverage, Spec §US1, Spec §US2, Spec §US3]
- [x] CHK020 Are requirements defined for an existing manual Argo CD installation that conflicts with repository-owned state? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK021 Are requirements defined for the transition from first-sync validation to automated reconciliation? [Coverage, Research §Validate First Handoff, Data Model §State Transitions]
- [x] CHK022 Are requirements defined for break-glass live changes and the repository backfill rule? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010, Contract §Failure Contract]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK023 Are missing-CRD, bad-repo-path, missing-credential, and partial-install failure requirements each documented with expected operator decisions? [Edge Case, Spec §US3, Quickstart §Recovery Rules]
- [x] CHK024 Is catalog unavailability or catalog version drift addressed as a requirements concern, not only as an implementation detail? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Research §Scaffold Catalog Modules]
- [x] CHK025 Are requirements present for avoiding destructive self-management behavior when the Argo CD source path is wrong? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Research §Validate First Handoff]
- [x] CHK026 Are rerun/idempotency expectations described clearly enough to prevent duplicate installation or duplicate Application resources? [Edge Case, Spec §SC-004]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK027 Are security requirements complete for raw secrets, repository credentials, provider credentials, and CI/CD injection boundaries? [Security, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK028 Are reliability requirements defined for recovery from partial bootstrap and for safe reapplication after convergence? [Reliability, Spec §US3, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK029 Are observability requirements specified for what operators must be able to inspect during handoff and recovery? [Gap, Spec §FR-008, Quickstart §Verify Handoff]
- [x] CHK030 Are maintainability requirements clear for future catalog module refreshes and reviewed source updates? [Maintainability, Research §Source The Terragrunt Catalog Module Remotely, Data Model §Catalog Module Source]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are all external dependencies documented, including Kubernetes access, remote state, provider credentials, chart source, catalog source, and repository access? [Dependency, Plan §Primary Dependencies, Contract §Required Preconditions]
- [x] CHK032 Are assumptions about `argocd` namespace, internal service exposure, and no public ingress explicit and traceable to requirements? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Research §No Public Ingress]
- [x] CHK033 Are external credential assumptions documented without making environment variables part of desired-state input requirements? [Assumption, Spec §FR-007, Contract §Committed Inputs]
- [x] CHK034 Are prerequisites separated from requirements so reviewers can tell what must already exist versus what this feature creates? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Contract §Required Preconditions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK035 Is there any unresolved conflict between one-command bootstrap and the requirement to validate first handoff before enabling automation? [Conflict, Spec §FR-003, Research §Validate First Handoff]
- [x] CHK036 Is the self-management source path specified enough to avoid ambiguity about which Argo CD resources are owned after handoff? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-005, Plan §Repository Paths]
- [x] CHK037 Are live rollout validation requirements clearly separated from requirements-quality checklist expectations? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Plan §Testing]
- [x] CHK038 Does the spec avoid using "Argo CD owns itself" as shorthand without defining the actual ownership boundary? [Ambiguity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §IAC-003]

## Notes

- This checklist tests whether the feature requirements are well-written and reviewable.
- It does not verify that Terragrunt, OpenTofu, Helm, Kubernetes, or Argo CD behavior works.
